papers.title: 『大手笹世／芝居小屋廻り絵双六（仮題）』について
東濃地⽅の地芝居を中⼼とした地域伝統⽂化継承のための資源記録保存プロジェクト 2024年度活動報告
papers.description: 出版タイプ： VoR; type:その他; This Tono project is one of the initiatives under the theme of "Sustainable Society." So far, the project has focused on Gomoza and the Iiji Furusato Folklore Museum in Iiji Town, Ena City, collecting records on the history and culture of ji-shibai (local traditional theater) in preparation for digital archiving. Specifically, the project has digitized approximately 150 VHS tapes containing performance recordings, deciphered and preserved documentary materials, and carried out mold prevention treatments. This fiscal year, in particular, efforts were made to photograph documentary materials. Moving forward, the project plans to digitize non-theatrical photographs, capture and organize large exhibits such as agricultural tools in the museum, and collaborate with students to design the exhibition layout.
